typecheck.command: xcodebuild build-for-testing -project OpenBurnBar.xcodeproj -scheme OpenBurnBar -destination "platform=macOS,arch=arm64" -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .spm-cache -derivedDataPath .derived-data/ci-typecheck CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

blockingIssues.description: BackfillSchedulerTests still validates runtime wiring via source-text scanning (`test_refreshAll_guardCondition_isPresent`) rather than executing UsageAggregator.refreshAll/runScheduledBackfillIfNeeded success/failure persistence paths, so required runtime integration coverage remains unmet.
BackfillCursorStore monotonic guard still permits backward movement through negative epsilon (`>= -monotonicEpsilon`) and widens tolerance to 1ms, violating strict no-backward progression semantics.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require explicit command-level evidence of required startup-context reads before allowing `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`, and enforce this during scrutiny checks.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Fix feature handoff for session `1c91c316-bba4-423b-9def-9124a1ff399b` marks followedProcedure true but verification commands omit mission/AGENTS/library read evidence.

rejectedObservations.observation: Shared architecture library content remains switcher-centric and not token-accounting focused.
